reviews.review_description: This game is great but can you please add a settings menu settings that would be in it would be a slider to decrease or increase enemy damage and another slider to decrease or increase the amount of damage we do. I would like this to be added because my brother loves your game but just finds it too hard he wants to defeat priscilla without taking any damage to get a starting weapon he had it once but then died. He knows about the difficulty knob but even on the easiest difficulty it's still too hard for him and many other people

this game made my heart POUND in a good way though. This game made want to come back for more and try to beat the game i was so close but so far from doing so. the different paths you take always makes me want to make some trouble because of what it could be. anyways this is a great game loved being here from almost the start of when the game released. this game also has some perrrrsonality.

To the devs:

I absolutely love ur game, it's truly incredible. What would make it even better is if there was a harder difficulty option. (I've already beaten it in full on mode multiple times, it's not hard.) Also there aren't a lot of weapon available and theyre all pretty similar. It would be great to have more weapons to make the game more replayable and exciting. Also I'm having issues with the turning knob to change difficulty. It's very difficult to turn and doesn't make sense. With that said, I still love the game how it is and am very excited to see multiplayer when it comes out! (the joint is already looking pretty good)
Thank you

Everything about this game is top-tier, from the gameplay to the interface to the soundfx. And the battles are pure adrenaline. The menacing grime music sets the mood brilliantly & the dialogue during battle is very encouraging & fun. UNDERDOGS is easily one of the most visceral & stylish experiences currently available for VR. The physics-based combat here is the gold standard. When you pilot your "Rilla" (a gorilla-like mech) you're in for a high-intensity workout, bruv! Whether you’re landing a massive haymaker on a robotic pit-dog or grabbing an enemy to slam them into a wall - or grinder - every hit feels heavy & incredibly satisfying. The locomotion (dragging yourself across the arena floor with your knuckles) is intuitive & adds to the primal, animalistic intensity of the fights. The UK-inspired "grime" soundtrack gets your heart racing the second the arena gates open. Between fights, the roguelike loop keeps things fresh with over 100 items & upgrades. You can go from a basic brawler to a specialized killing machine with chainsaws, shock gauntlets & heavy plating. Underdogs is a masterclass in VR game design. It’s brutal, addictive & dripping with atmosphere. It's stacked with so many great great features: a unique & stylish comic-book aesthetic, deep roguelike mechanics with high replayability & one of the best soundtracks in VR. You can tell the devs have passion for this sweet, sweat-inducing game ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

When I got the game, I had absolutely no idea what to expect. And now, after a year, I can say for a fact that this is my favorite single-player game! The unique fighting mechanics for each weapon combo, the genuine effort you have to put in, and the world-building are all so original.
I've been playing for about 4 hours each week, but I still haven't discovered everything!

The thing I like the most besides the game itself is it's really helped with my arm strength and stability, both of which help me around the house.
Great game, would give it 8 stars if I could!

Is what a person with zero taste in games would say, this game is awsome. One thing is that it's hard to see through all the UI, but that's what makes it hard and fun. So get it, now, what are you waiting for? stop reading and get the game. Oh it's expensive? i don't care get NOW

I HAVE BEEN LOOKING FOR A MECH FIGHTING GAME FOR SSSSOOOO LONG UNTIL I FOUND UNDERDOGS. I mean it glitches once in a while but a side from that 150/10. The cutscenes are cool too. So if you like physics based combat like Hard Bullet or Blades & Sorcery, THIS IS YOU'RE GAME BRUVVA.
Its a Rougelike so you will die alot but its SO good i would have paid full price for it (got it 30% off)

I have been playing this game since 2024 and I have a badge to prove it, the community is better then most games on VR and the game itself? It’s other worldly this game makes you work for your cash and work for your upgrades so if you don’t like having a blast and getting rewarded it’s not for you.

For only 30 dollars, you and your brother are trying to head into a city to save him.. YOU.. are piloting a roguelike, motion copying, 5 ton, metal mech, while he is giving you heads up on everything. You have to fight robot dogs,chainsaw dogs,bosses and even more bosses.. there are endless weapons ranging from fingers to rip your enemies apart.. to a literall wrecking ball.. this game is insanely replayable, since of the roguelike mechanic.. plus as a new vr player.. YOU DONT GET MOTION SICK!! WOHOO.. plus there is an entire story.. buy this game.. NOW

reviews.review_title: Very fun, like by a lot.
reviews.review_description:
I like that you can rip things in half and probably do several safety hazards by throwing the dead bots into the crowd. I also like just fighting a bunch of stuff in general, I did think it was really hard, and the only having one life before you have to start over was bad, but then I kept trying, and now it feels very easy to get through the campaign

One thing I don't like about it though is that there isn't a free play mode for the campaign, I would like to be able to fight in the battles and just purely resort to ripping even the bosses apart like it's basically nothing. But that is just a small thing, it still is a very good game.
Beat robot dog to a pulp yes
